g.text: COMMIT WAIT: pause until TT.now().earliest > commit_ts
Client gets ack. By now, true time > commit_ts everywhere.

text.tspan: u2192 actual time is somewhere in this interval
All replicas agree to commit at this timestamp via Paxos.
At this point, the 'commit' has been agreed,
but not yet acknowledged to the client.
Wait until time has definitely advanced past +u03b5.
During this 1u20137 ms wait, the leader does no work u2014
but the txn is durable in Paxos already.
Uncertainty u03b5 is SMALL because Spanner uses:
u2022 GPS receivers in every datacenter
u2022 NTP-style protocol with bounded uncertainty
any action observed by clients AFTER the ack
This enables LINEARIZABLE reads + EXTERNALLY CONSISTENT writes
at global scale u2014 without expensive cross-DC coordination per read.
